item.title: ++i or i++
item.description: Who is faster and more efficient: i++ or ++i?! Let’s take an example for better understanding: int i = 0 ; int j = i++; // j here is equal to 0 still because the increment happens after the return of the value // This means that the old value is kept in a copy. int k = ++i ; // k here is equal to 2 because it was incremented without a copy Therefore, ++i is more efficient to use than i++.

item.title: About
item.description: I am a computer science graduate from AUC with minor in History with the help of the prestegious Tomorrow’s Leaders Scholarship Program. I also spent a semester abroad at JMU in Virginia, USA. I am open to kick start my career in tech. If you want to know more about me, I would be happy to chat with you.

item.title: Contact
item.description: Find Me on the Web Twitter Github Linkedin Facebook Email Email: mlamine.abdellaoui@aucegypt.edu

item.title: Resources
item.description: I intend to put anything I came across and found it useful for public knowledge. To be updated soon.
